summary: Every human-facing surface leads with the decision layer — verdict first in plain language, detail on demand — and a verbose surface that does not state the verdict is a defect, not thoroughness.

rules.rule: Lead every human-facing surface with a one-line verdict in plain language (are we OK, and how many things need action), passing the one-glance test before any detail.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: Give each red item an actionable next step, not just a failure marker — a red that does not say what to do is half a defect report.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: Verbosity must never be the default view — detail lives behind a flag, toggle, or drill-in (--verbose, expand, show raw), pulled on demand.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: Never let skipped, not-run, or not-applicable items be simply absent — they are stated explicitly, because absence reads as fine and lies by omission.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: Never ship a verbose-but-mute surface (a wall the human must read end-to-end to find the one thing that matters); the cure is to invert the order — verdict at the top, depth underneath — not to append a summary at the bottom.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate
